fields.value:
Christian Burial is an Easter liturgy as the BCP says, and it finds it’s meaning in the resurrection.  In this workshop we will be discussing how to help ensure that is what we communicate and invite people to experience, and we will offer some practical ideas and forms about how to do that, both in the moment and in advance.  Discuss how the time of death, planning for burial and the burial liturgy are a key pastoral and evangelical moment—even more than marriage!
II.	We live in a culture that doesn’t like to acknowledge death, and that treats life after death very differently than the promises of scripture.  We aren’t going to cover it in depth today, it is important to understand that teaching and encouraging people to plan for their own death is a Christian responsibility.  The BCP requires that congregation’ be reminded annually that each person should have a will.  There are many ways to handle inviting pre-planning, and there will be workshop’s this spring. . .
III.	Go over some scripture connected to resurrection—talk about having an adult forum to talk about these and the passages suggested for burial—ask people to name passages they love and how that fits in what they might want for their own service.
IV.	Go over the “Concerning the Service” page (BCP 490). How is your parish teaching and living up to these instructions?  Do they make sense to you?  For example, do you ask congregation members to attend funerals?  Do you livestream them?  Do you work with families intentionally about scheduling?  Do you have a parish care group?  What might you suggest for your congregation?
V.	Go over sample instruction sheet to give to local funeral homes
i.	Talk about other practical procedural concerns
1.	How does someone contact the parish, how do you communicate that you cannot necessarily accommodate and or provide music when it is scheduled without consultation
VI.	Go over simple sample planning sheet
i.	Talk about music complexities
ii.	Suggest a hymn sing in churches with musicians where people can name some favorite hymns to be kept on file
VII.	Talk about shape and journey of liturgy—ask people about moments they have found inspiring or transformative at burial services
i.	Talk about common pitfalls such as too many or poorly prepared eulogies
ii.	Talk about lay pastoral leaders and worship leaders being able to do funerals—what that looks likes--get people trained from your congress

fields.value:
The parish of St. Matthew’s seeks a multifaceted spiritual leader who leads with humility and love while navigating the administrative realities of an active, program-centered parish. We value servant leadership—leading through service rather than authority—and the ability to collaborate effectively with lay leaders and staff. We hope to call an energetic rector who intends to make St. Matthew’s and Hillsborough their home rather than a temporary stop.
Like many congregations, St. Matthew’s has an aging membership, which creates significant pastoral care needs. While we have strong lay leadership supporting pastoral care ministries, there remains a deep desire for personal pastoral presence from the rector through visits, spiritual counsel, and relational connection. The next rector will need to balance delegation with maintaining meaningful pastoral relationships. We also hope for a priest who can help us continue to welcome and engage younger adults and families. A sense of humor and the ability to connect across generations are highly valued.
St. Matthew’s is also seeking a capable organizational leader who is comfortable with administrative responsibilities. Our rector should be technologically proficient and able to navigate Zoom worship and other digital communication platforms. Because we value continuity in ministry, we hope for a collaborative team player who will work closely with our associate rector and staff, taking time to understand the parish before initiating major changes.
We are an engaged and thoughtful worshiping community that places great importance on preaching. We seek a preacher who centers the Gospel while remaining attentive to the wider context of Christian morality, our parish’s history of enslavement and racial injustice, and the realities of contemporary life. Our next rector should articulate a hopeful vision for the parish and communicate that vision clearly through preaching, teaching, and conversation.
Finally, we seek a rector with emotional maturity who can navigate stress, conflict, and differing perspectives with wisdom and grace. Our parish includes a variety of backgrounds and viewpoints, and we value leaders who foster healthy dialogue while maintaining clear expectations and boundaries when needed. Above all, we hope for a rector who sees every person as precious and worthy of care, modeling that spirit of compassion for the entire community.
Resume, cover letter (addressed to Search Committee), OTM or Vocation Hub profile uploaded here: https://episdionc.formstack.com/forms/applications

fields.value:
The parish of St. Matthew’s seeks a multifaceted spiritual leader who leads with humility and love and can manage the administrative realities of an active, program-centered parish. We value servant leadership—leading through service rather than authority. Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to collaborate with lay leaders and staff are essential. We hope to call an energetic rector who intends to make St. Matthew’s and Hillsborough their home, not a brief stop along the way.
Like many churches, we have an aging congregation with significant pastoral care needs. While lay leaders provide strong support, parishioners also desire individual pastoral presence from the rector through visits and spiritual counsel. We seek a priest who can delegate effectively while maintaining meaningful personal connections. We also hope to attract and serve younger parishioners and families. A sense of humor and the ability to connect across generations are important gifts.
Our rector must be comfortable with administrative and executive responsibilities. Technological competence is necessary, including navigating Zoom worship and multiple communication platforms. We value continuity with our associate rector and staff and seek a collaborative leader willing to listen, learn the parish culture, and build trust before making major changes.
St. Matthew’s is an engaged and thoughtful worshipping community that values preaching. We seek a sensitive preacher rooted in the Gospel, avoiding narrow or personal agendas while addressing Christian morality, our parish’s history of enslavement and racial injustice, current events, and today’s political climate with care and balance. Our next rector should articulate a compelling vision for our future and communicate it clearly through sermons, forums, and programs.
Emotional maturity and resilience are essential. Our parish includes diverse backgrounds and perspectives, and our priest must navigate conflict constructively and diplomatically. We value clear, compassionate communication and the ability to facilitate healthy conversation. At the same time, the rector must set appropriate expectations and boundaries when needed.
Above all, we seek a rector who sees every person as precious and worthy of time, attention, and love—and models that vision for the whole congregation.
Resume, OTM Portfolio, and cover letter addressed to the Search Committee uploaded here: https://episdionc.formstack.com/forms/applications

fields.value:
The St. Michael’s rectory, two blocks from the church, is a 4-bedroom, 3-bath single-family home built in 1939. The rectory sits on a quiet corner in a lovely neighborhood lined with mature trees. Inside features a newly renovated kitchen and a charming living room with a wood-burning fireplace. A large deck and adjoining screened-in porch provide a lovely space to relax and entertain. In addition to the new kitchen, recent renovations include a new roof, tree maintenance, and a new storage shed.
Residency in the rectory is optional, though most rectors have lived there.

fields.value:
Founded in 1859, Trinity Episcopal Church is a parish with deep roots and an open heart. For more than a century and a half, we have gathered as a diverse and welcoming community, believing that church is a place where everyone is known, valued, and invited into a relationship with God and one another. That belief is visible each time we worship together, especially when we share the Peace, not as a brief liturgical moment, but as a genuine exchange of connection, reunion, and joy. At Trinity, we strive to live the Gospel fully, swift to love and bold to live.

Trinity is a lay-centered parish, shaped by collaboration, transparency, and a strong sense that “we are in this together.” Our worship life centers on the Rite Two Eucharist, offered on Sunday mornings at 8:30 and 11:00, with music and a choir enriching the later service. A quieter Wednesday noon healing service provides space for prayer, reflection, and restoration. Worship is rooted in the Book of Common Prayer and is reverent, warm, meaningful, and deeply human.

Preaching matters to us. We value sermons that step out from behind the pulpit and speak to people in a relational, grounded, real-life way, lightly held rather than read. A sense of humor is welcome, not for performance, but because joy and authenticity help the Gospel take root.

In recent years, Trinity has demonstrated resilience and strong stewardship. We completed more than $200,000 in deferred maintenance from the sale of the Grey House, including upgrades to HVAC, electrical, water, and technology systems. With maintenance reserves and service contracts now in place, our campus is well cared for and positioned to support ministry for the long term.

To discern with us, please submit a Letter expressing interest, resume, OTM/Vocational Hub Profile (if available), and a sermon recording to the Discernment Committee via the Rev. Will Dickinson, diocesan Transition Minister:

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value:
As Trinity looks to the future, we pray for a collaborative leader who values lay leadership and shared ministry.  The congregation identified four steady gifts they desire, qualities that feel less like a “job description” and more like the kind of shepherd a parish family can trust.

First, trustworthy integrity: someone ethical, respectful, and confidential, who leads with transparency and earns confidence through quiet consistency. Second, genuine connection: a Rector who listens well, communicates clearly, and builds relationships beyond the sermon, someone comfortable with people of every age, including our acolytes and youth. Third, spiritual depth with real-world wisdom: a leader grounded in theology who can preach with relevance and efficiency, and who understands the secular world while helping us apply faith to everyday life, even when situations are not black and white. And fourth, mission-driven leadership: a Rector who honors Trinity’s history in Manassas and Prince William County, respects the strong teams already in place, and guides growth through pastoral care, formation, outreach, music ministry, and traditional liturgy.

Through prayer, conversation, and shared discernment, the Trinity congregation presents this profile as an expression of who we are and the rector we seek. Trinity is ready to welcome a rector who will walk with us into the next chapter of faithful, joyful, and Spirit-led ministry.

fields.value: Christ Episcopal Church is a small, vibrant, and welcoming parish located in the heart of downtown Indiana, Pennsylvania. Our church community is situated in a picturesque university town nestled in the Allegheny mountains, approximately 60 miles from Pittsburgh. We are seeking a priest-in-charge who will actively support our ongoing spiritual journey and provide leadership as we strive to share God’s love through acts of service to others.

If you feel called to enter into discernment with Christ Church, please submit the following materials via email to the Rev. Kimberly Karashin, Interim Transition Minister, at kkarashin@episcopalpgh.org
•	Resume/CV
•	Updated OTM or Vocation Hub profile
•	Link to online sermon(s)
•	Letter of interest describing why you feel called to apply for the position and the gifts you will bring to the role

fields.label: Applicant narrative questions
fields.value: 1. How do you approach pastoral care in your ministry?, 5. What has been your experience leading change in the Church?, 10. What theological convictions shape your ministry?

Additional financial information can be found in the 2025 Parochial Report and Annual Parish Meeting Report located with other search materials on Christ Church's website, https://christchurchipa.org/.

fields.label: Details
fields.value:
Compensation and benefits are negotiable.  The details provided in this section represent what is included in a typical Letter of Agreement for a part-time priest in the Diocese of Pittsburgh.

According to our diocesan compensation guide, the absolute minimum for this half-time position is $33,500.  The recommended compensation for a priest with 5-9 years of ordained experience would be $34,500-$43,000.  For a priest with 10-14 years experience, it would be $38,000-$43,000.  For the complete compensation scale, see our diocesan compensation guide at https://www.episcopalpgh.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/ClergyCompGuide2026-Final-for-Approval.pdf.  The figures in the scale represent compensation for full-time work (and so must be halved) and INCLUDE housing and SECA reimbursement.

fields.label: Details
fields.value: At the priest's request, a portion of their salary may be designated as “Housing Allowance” under the Federal Internal Revenue Code.  This is not a separate amount.

fields.label: Details
fields.value: The compensation figures included in the diocesan guidelines are meant to include SECA, so there is generally not a separate SECA designation.  Any need related to SECA reimbursement can be considered in the negotiation of the overall compensation.

fields.label: Details
fields.value: The diocese's medical benefits guide can be found at https://www.episcopalpgh.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/10/MedicalBenefitsGuide2026-Final-Approved.pdf.

fields.label: Vacation & Leave Details
fields.value: Generally, half-time priests receive one month (96 hours) of annual leave, which includes at least four Sundays.

fields.label: Continuing Education Details
fields.value: Generally, professional development leave is at the rate of two weeks per year, for classes, conferences, etc., that are expected to be mutually beneficial to both the priest and the parish.  The parish has set aside $400 of continuing education funding in its budget.  The Diocese makes available $500 of grant funding for continuing education per clergyperson, per year.

fields.label: Sabbatical Provision Details
fields.value: One week (24 hours) per year of service in this parish for sabbatical leave, to be available after the fifth year, up to a maximum of seven (7) weeks.  Sabbatical arrangements will be made in full consultation with the Vestry, to ensure benefits for the parish and the priest. Further, a written sabbatical plan must be submitted to the Bishop for approval no less than 90 days before the sabbatical is scheduled to commence. Any calculated sabbatical time that is unused at the time of departure from the parish will be forfeited, with no compensation equivalency permitted.

fields.label: Travel/Auto Account Details
fields.value:
The Vestry will pay for travel expenses incurred by the priest in fulfilling the duties of the office, including:

a)  Mileage, at the current rate provided by the Internal Revenue Service, to be reviewed annually against the updated IRS rate.  (This does not include the cost of commuting to and from the parish.) 
b)	Parking fees, tolls, bus fares, etc.

fields.label: Equipment/Phone Details
fields.value: The Vestry will pay for reasonable costs associated with either residential telephone service or cell phone usage, depending on the priest's preferred method of communication for ministry purposes.  In either case, the telephone number will be published to insure the Priest-in-Charge’s ready accessibility in case of emergencies.

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value: The priest-in-charge at Christ Episcopal Church will be expected to serve as a dynamic worship leader who values and preserves the parish’s traditional worship style while also inspiring the congregation to consider contemporary or blended formats when appropriate. This role requires a steadfast commitment to inclusive ministry, ensuring that all individuals feel welcomed into the church community. The ideal candidate will bring enthusiasm and energy to help grow parish membership and will guide the congregation in finding new and meaningful ways to live out their faith within Indiana, Pennsylvania. Recognizing the importance of pastoral care, the priest-in-charge will provide compassionate support to parishioners who are homebound, ill, or elderly. Strong administrative skills are essential for the effective management of parish operations. Additionally, the priest-in-charge will be dedicated to Christian formation for both adults and children, fostering spiritual growth across all age groups. By encouraging and nurturing the diverse talents and abilities of parishioners, the priest-in-charge will ensure that everyone’s gifts are recognized and utilized in service to both the church and the wider community.

fields.label: Ministry skills  (maximum of 10)
fields.value: Adult Formation, Campus Ministry, Children & Family Formation, Church Growth, Community Building, Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, Pastoral Care, Worship & Liturgy

fields.value:
Trinity Parish seeks a visionary rector to lead our urban, multicultural and multiracial congregation in Wilmington, Delaware.

We are two locations, four weekend worship services, and two languages—united in faith, liturgy, and service. Our historic mother church, Old Swedes, anchors our spiritual heritage, while Trinity Church serves as our primary location with three Sunday services and a thriving Spanish-language community.

Who We Are: A welcoming parish celebrating diversity across many ages, cultures, races, and economic backgrounds. We embrace all people—regardless of ability, gender identity, sexual orientation, family structure, or nationality—who seek God's love.

What We Seek: A lively, engaging preacher who connects scripture to contemporary issues and our lives. A leader who will:
•	Strengthen Latino participation in parish leadership and ministries
•	Champion compassionate social justice and community outreach
•	Develop programs for children, young people, and young families
•	Foster collaboration between our Anglo and Latino congregations
•	Support stewardship of Old Swedes and partnerships with the National Parks Service

Our Location: The Wilmington area offers a wealth of activities and historic sites, making it a vibrant place to live and worship. Major urban hubs, beautiful beaches, and picturesque mountains are all within a two-hour drive.

fields.value:
St. John’s Episcopal Church, a vibrant and historic parish in downtown Youngstown, seeks a full-time Priest-in-Charge to lead us into our next chapter of faithful ministry. Deeply committed to inclusion, artistic expression, and community partnership, St. John’s is a welcoming, outward-facing parish eager to grow in vitality and impact.

The Priest-in-Charge will serve as pastor, spiritual leader, and chief collaborator—guiding worship, nurturing discipleship, and equipping lay leaders for shared ministry. This priest will build on St. John’s strong foundation of engaged laity, compassionate outreach, and creative liturgical life, while helping the congregation clarify vision, strengthen stewardship, and deepen relationships within the wider community.

Primary responsibilities include:

Providing inspiring, grounded preaching and sacramental leadership.

Offering attentive pastoral care in collaboration with established lay ministries.

Fostering inclusive Christian formation for all ages.

Encouraging artistic and musical expression as integral to worship and parish identity.

Strengthening partnerships in the Youngstown community and within the Diocese of Ohio.

Supporting effective administration, financial stewardship, and strategic planning alongside parish leadership.

St. John’s seeks a priest who listens well, communicates transparently, and leads collaboratively. The ideal candidate will value diverse perspectives, honor the parish’s rich history, and inspire confidence as we grow into a renewed season of shared purpose. A heart for inclusion—including LGBTQ+ affirmation—and a passion for engaging younger generations are essential.

With a strong lay leadership base, established outreach ministries, and a deep commitment to being a beacon of hope in downtown Youngstown, St. John’s is ready for a priest who will help us live more fully into God’s unfolding vision for our future.
Send OTM, resume, cover letter to the Rev. Canon Jessie Gutgsell Dodson, Canon to the Ordinary. Jdodson@dohio.org

fields.value:
The Parish of St. Mary & St. Jude’s is financially healthy.  Regular offertory income and
designated funds have covered our operating expenses for more than 10 years, allowing us to
maintain and grow our ministries, staff, and facilities, as well as a short-term cushion for unexpected
costs.  Pledge fulfillment is generally consistent, though Seasonal fluctuations and an aging donor
base have made forecasting less predictable.
 
In addition to offertory income, we are blessed to have a number of endowments
designated for specific purposes:
-          The Bishop Doane Endowment provides a monthly stipend that contributes
to our operating budget;
-          The Foulke Fund and the St. Jude’s Fund are used specifically for
church building and maintenance;
-          The Leadership fund was established to assist in enriching the missions of
the parish;
-          The Stroud Fund provides money for a Rector’s Discretionary Fund, as well
as a quarterly stipend that is disbursed to various community non-profits via a
grant application process; and
-          Finally, the Centennial Fund is available for larger parish projects, subject to
the approval of its trustees.
We maintain transparency with monthly financial reports. We have a bookkeeper,
finance committee and Vestry that review our operating accounts as well as our
endowments.
 
We are seeking a full-time Priest and compensation will be based on the guidelines that
are outlined in the Diocese of Maine 2025 Policy Resolution 4 as a base salary. Salary
will be based in part on the applicant’s education and experience, and compensation
will include health insurance, dental insurance and an 18% contribution to the pension
plan. We also provide a rectory that is semi furnished. We are fortunate to have the
financial strength and stability to attractively compensate our new Priest.

fields.label: Details
fields.value: The 4 to 5 bedroom, 2 full bath rectory is located in Northeast Harbor's village, next door to the Parish Center and a one block walk to St. Mary's-by-the-Sea, the Gothic cathedral style stone church.

fields.label: Details
Vacation & Leave Details
fields.value: A minimum annual vacation of 30 calendar days, four of which are Sundays.

fields.label: Continuing Education Details
fields.value: Time and financial assistance shall be provided for continuing education consisting of a minimum of 10 days and $750 per year.

fields.label: Sabbatical Provision Details
fields.value: Each presbyter canonically resident in the Diocese shall, following each five-year period of service, be eligible for a leave of absence for four months for spiritual and intellectual refreshment and shall be entitled to full compensation during such period of leave, provided however, that no presbyter shall be eligible for such leave of absence unless he or she has served in the position from which he or she would take leave for at least three years. This leave may not be used at the time of termination of employment or retirement, nor may it be accrued. If questions shall arise concerning the proper interpretation of this section, the parties shall consult with the Bishop and the decision of the Bishop shall be final and binding on all concerned parties.

fields.label: Travel/Auto Account Details
fields.value: Mileage for church purposes shall be reimbursed at the current IRS rate.

fields.label: Other Benefits or Comments
fields.value:
Housing Equity Allowance:
Clergy who live in housing provided by the congregation shall be paid a housing equity allowance equal to at least 3% of the Total Clergy Compensation. These monies shall be invested in a tax-sheltered investment account that becomes the property of the clergy person after one year of service.

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value:
We seek a Rector whose preaching serves as a bridge, thoughtfully interpreting Holy Scripture to
illuminate the complexities of our everyday Christian lives. Our unique context requires a leader who
is as comfortable engaging with our vibrant summer community—which draws residents from across
the globe—as they are with the "hardy and faithful" few who sustain the parish during the quiet,
demanding Maine winters. We need a Person who has the ability and interest to maintain and grow
the momentum during the quiet season.
Our vision for this next chapter focuses on intergenerational growth. While we cherish our dedicated
older members, we are eager for gentle and innovative leadership to help us connect with the families
and youth of Mount Desert Island. We seek a rector with the creative energy to design services and
programs that resonate with younger generations, transforming our sanctuary into a space where all
ages feel a sense of belonging year-round.
The ideal candidate will inherit a legacy of robust outreach and strong ecumenical ties, particularly
with our three neighboring Episcopal parishes. We are looking for a collaborative and consensus-
building leader to sustain a supportive Vestry, our weekly Zoom Bible studies and our deeply valued
tradition of pastoral care. Thus, ensuring that every member of our community—whether local or
seasonal—feels known, nurtured, and inspired to serve.  We are interested in having a leader with
technical knowledge to help us bridge the distance for our seasonal members and those unable to
attend in person.

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value:
The rector will serve as the parish’s spiritual leader, guiding worship and preaching that is reverent, accessible, and rooted in scripture. Sermons are expected to be engaging, concise, and relatable, helping parishioners connect biblical teaching to their daily lives while consistently affirming God’s unconditional love and grace. A strong understanding of Episcopal liturgy and sacramental life is essential, as is a deep respect for the traditions that define St. Peter’s worship. At the same time, the parish welcomes thoughtful enrichment of worship that speaks to a broad range of ages without compromising its core identity.

Pastoral care is a central expectation of this ministry as is the task of equipping and encouraging lay leaders to share in the ministry of caring. Strong relationships and community engagement are essential to the health of the parish. The rector should be visible, approachable, and able to connect easily with children, youth, adults, and seniors alike. A warm personality, genuine empathy, and a sense of humor are highly valued. The parish hopes for a Rector who actively supports parish ministries, including collaboration with the preschool, and who fosters a welcoming, joyful atmosphere in which all feel accepted, valued, and encouraged to grow in faith.

fields.value:
Cople Parish is a warm and loving Body of Christ devoted to a shared love of the teachings of the Gospel. A sense of community is at the forefront of what makes Cople Parish home for our congregants and we are actively focused on growing in that way. We are a group of people who walk into our worship spaces happy to be there. 
Established in 1664, our Parish has three historic church buildings: St. James and Yeocomico, which have services most Sundays, and Nomini, which hosts occasional services. We have one Vestry and one budget. Though many parishioners worship at one church or the other, what brings us all together is our shared intention to live out the Great Commandment. 
Each of the church buildings in our Parish stand in pastoral settings overlooking our local waterways, farm fields and woodlands. The peacefulness of these settings, looking upon the water from St. James’ and into the woods and fields at Yeocomico, reminds us of the beauty God has bestowed upon the earth. Our parishioners feel that there are strength and comfort to be found for all who attend and visit our Parish in these rural spaces and we enjoy sharing them with the broader community. 

We are seeking a Rector who:  
1. Can provide pastoral care to and connect with people in all seasons of life, from children to the elderly. 
2. Has strong spiritual and preaching/teaching skills, able to give sermons that people can understand and apply to their own lives.
3. Has the necessary skills and will be happy to help us develop a vision, mission, and strategy for how to steward our three historic church buildings and two congregations in a changing world while becoming a more tight-knit community.  We endeavor to find a new season of ministry where we all work together to develop a vibrant future for Cople Parish.
4. Can help us learn how to develop and deepen broader community relationships and invite more organizations, people, and events in our spaces.

To apply, please email a letter expressing interest, OTM profile/Vocation Hub profile (if either is available), and resume to the Contact below:

Questions may be forwarded to the Rev. Will Dickinson, Diocesan Transition Minister, at wdickinson@episcopalvirginia.org

Since 1907, the community that cares about Cople has expressed that by establishing funds that contribute to the parish’s welfare. Our various funds have an overall value of $3.6 mil. Some are restricted to using a yearly distribution only and for a few we can use the principal if necessary. We are also fortunate to have a group of experts in the congregation to help maintain, support, and make use of these funds.

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value:
Cople Parish faces challenges that are not unusual for a rural parish community. Like many churches we have struggled with attendance due to an aging population (the median age in our county is 50). However, our area is attractive to retirees. Those newcomers have been and can continue to be a source of new parishioners. And we do have some very active younger members. The members of our congregation are dedicated to the working of the church however we do not have a deep bench regarding leadership and some members are taking on multiple responsibilities.

Our outreach efforts significantly serve our neighbors, but they don’t often draw folks into our parish. We hope our incoming Rector will be able to help us learn to make deeper connections in the local community and think of new ways to invite folks into our spaces and to help us develop leaders for the next generation.

 St. James’ was built in the 1890s as an ecumenical mission chapel for the then remote village of Tidwells (a very small and isolated village populated by watermen on Lower Machodoc Creek, close to the Potomac River). Nomini Church, on the banks of Nomini Creek and the third church that bears this name, was built in the mid-nineteenth century. The original Yeocomico Church was built as a chapel-of-ease in 1655. It is believed that that original wooden structure is within the walls of the 1706 brick structure that stands today. Yeocomico Church is one of the oldest active places of worship in the United States spanning over 370 years of history. With a Declaration of Independence signer having been a member of our parish, it reminds us of the importance of our past. We embrace the responsibility of the stewardship of our historic churches, but we understand and experience that having three churches also poses a challenge when decisions are made regarding where to hold services and other gatherings. Some parishioners identify with one church building over another. These are issues we are willing to take an honest look at for the betterment of our whole parish family.

We are looking for a new Rector who can help us to develop clear and concise vision and mission statements and produce a strategic plan that will help us to determine the best use of our many buildings for a new season of ministry. We believe this will help encourage our members to continue their good work while also bringing new people into the fold.

fields.value:
Holy Cross Episcopal Church in Hixson, TN, just outside of Chattanooga, is looking for our next Priest-in-Charge! Hixson is a rapidly growing suburban area just outside of Chattanooga, and we are looking to grow with our community.
We are looking for a spiritual leader, who also plans to join us as a partner in ministry. Our culture is one of collegiality—we want you to bring your values and to appreciate ours. The next Priest-in-Charge of Holy Cross will find people willing to serve and looking for guidance and motivation in how best to do so. As one of our Vestry members put it, “There are a lot of big ‘Christ hearts’ in this parish. What we need is someone to help direct them.”
We recently completed a congregation-wide conversation geared toward creating our new mission statement: “Rooted in the love of Christ, we are a welcoming, inclusive, and joyful community where all belong. Through worship, service, and fellowship, we embrace tradition with a progressive spirit—striving always to love our neighbors as ourselves.”
Holy Cross is the newest parish in the Diocese of East Tennessee. Our parish was formed out of the merger of two existing parishes in the Chattanooga area. As such, our “DNA” contains elements of each of those parishes, as well as a strand that is uniquely us--who we are now, rooted in the present and looking toward the future.

If you feel that you may be called to serve as Priest-in-Charge of Holy Cross, please submit a letter of interest, along with your resume or CV, a copy of your OTM portfolio, and recordings of two recent sermons to the Rev. Canon Joe Woodfin, jwoodfin@dioet.org.

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value:
Primary Duties and Responsibilities
Parishioner Outreach & Connection
• Welcome Events: Plan and organize newcomer welcome events.
• Parish-wide Events: Plan, promote, and implement parish-wide fellowship events,
including high-profile services such as Trinity Sunday, as well as seasonal, social, and
community-building gatherings.
• Newcomer Protocol: Create, implement, and manage a structured "Welcoming to
Connection" protocol, ensuring visitors are greeted, followed up with, and welcomed into the
community.
• Tracking & Assimilation: Create and manage a "connection" protocol, using the
church database to track parishioner/newcomer interactions ("touches") to identify engagement
gaps and encourage deepening participation.
• Sunday Presence: Participate in Sunday service welcome and greeting, acting as a
visible, relational presence in the Narthex and at coffee hour.
Pastoral Care & Community
• Senior Engagement: Manage senior population care and engagement programs,
ensuring connection with homebound members and developing social/spiritual opportunities for
older adults.
• WOTC Liaison: Serve as primary staff liaison to the Women of the Church
(WOTC) or equivalent groups (e.g., Helping Hands, Pastoral Care teams) to align outreach
efforts with parish goals.
Volunteer & Committee Leadership
• Committee Leadership: Lead the Engagement Committee, focusing on strategic,
long-term initiatives for parish growth and community building.
• Volunteer Support: Manage a comprehensive volunteer recognition and support
program, identifying talents, providing training, and ensuring volunteer appreciation.
Qualifications & Skills
• Theological Alignment: A desire to serve in an Episcopal Church context, with
understanding or willingness to learn Episcopal liturgy and welcoming traditions.
• Relational Skills: Strong interpersonal skills; ability to make people feel seen,
welcomed, and valued.
• Administration: Experience with church database management (e.g., Realm,
Servant Keeper) and tracking, or capability to learn these systems rapidly.
• Event Management: Demonstrated ability to plan, organize, and execute events
from conception to completion.
• Volunteer Management: Experience recruiting, training, and supporting lay
volunteers.
• Leadership: Ability to lead teams and collaborate with clergy, staff, and vestry.
Reports to Interim Rector or Rector.

gift was added to our reserve funds in the Diocesan Investment Trust (DIT) – funds invested by our diocese and
individual congregations and managed by TIAA with oversight from diocesan Trustees on behalf of those investors. 2025 was a challenging year, but God continues to be faithful. We withdrew $80,000 from our DIT reserve fund to cover a budgeted deficit. We ended 2025 with a cash balance of $14,722 in our cash accounts. Even after thewithdrawal of money from our DIT reserve fund, the $25,000 bequest and stock market gains resulted in only a minimal decrease in that fund for the year of $3,768. We have a Vestry-approved deficit budget for 2026, and we are trusting God that we will be able to do all that we have been called to do this year.

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value:
St. Paul’s is a small congregation with a big vision to follow in Jesus' footsteps, feed the hungry, care for our neighbors, and spread unconditional love. We prioritize worship, creative expression, and radical hospitality. Being a “family-sized” congregation blesses us with the ability to know and grow close to one another. We openly share thoughts and ideas during study, when facing challenging circumstances, or making difficult decisions. We are a mixed bag professionally, socioeconomically, racially, sexual orientation, etc. This blesses us with a variety of spiritual gifts, personalities, and leadership abilities. We are committed to serving our Carondelet community, where there is much need. This keeps us busy and engaged, but we would benefit from more opportunities to have fun and spend time with one another. St. Paul’s is a small congregation with minimal support staff, so we have experienced “burn out” in the past. We learned to be realistic about our expectations and demands placed on our priest and one another. We added apart-time Administrator and increased our bookkeeper’s hours so our priest can focus on priestly ministry. Setting attainable and sustainable goals is an important aspect of planning our programs and events. We desire a strong preacher and teacher, a pastor who can appreciate our quirkiness and uniqueness, a collaborative leader A leader who shares responsibilities when appropriate and leads us in discernment(not just an “ideas” person.) Someone who is a good listener and encourages us to bring creative ideas to the table. Someone who will foster broader community involvement to connect us with other congregations in Carondelet, our Convocation and our Diocese to partner in ministry and support one another in serving our
neighbors. We are stronger together! We want an organized and focused leader who provides guidance and organization to keep us focused on ministry and program priorities.

Thanks to strong financial controls and careful budgeting, St. Andrew’s is in a good financial position. We generally come very close to meeting or exceeding the full amount pledged over the course of the year. This was true in 2023 and 2024. For 2025, fulfillment was 94% of $412,344 pledged. Annual expenses are largely fixed, including clergy and staff salaries and benefits, diocesan assessment, insurance, utilities, building maintenance, administrative expenses, and program costs. We finished 2025 with a surplus, and the 2026 budget projects a small deficit. 

Two recent significant contributions to our financial base, a generous gift and the sale of an adjacent property, along with $480,000 in endowments provide breathing room and further opportunities to live out our mission to welcome others to our community and to follow Christ’s example of service to each other and our community.   

St. Andrew’s has no mortgages or liens. We recently completed a campus renovation project with a $1.5 million capital campaign.

fields.value:
St. Andrew’s looks forward to a strong and loving relationship with our new rector. We have
identified important areas that a candidate ideally would provide.
1. Inspire us spiritually and bring energy to the congregation through preaching in ways
that make us think, telling us stories and helping us connect the Gospel and scriptures to
our daily lives; meeting with parishioners for spiritual guidance and encouragement; and
supporting our traditional music ministry. The new rector will continue to sustain our
thoughtful use of liturgy, helping us develop ways to be our best selves. We seek to love
God and love our neighbors as ourselves, living out our Baptismal Covenant.
2. The new rector will help us explore and implement ways to grow our congregation. We
can invite people in, reconnect with the St. Francis House Episcopal Student Center
adjacent to the UW-Madison campus, with whom we have had a long-standing
relationship, and provide activities that invite our neighbors in. We are open to new and
innovative ideas. In order to include more youth, we need to consider activities that suit
their needs and interests. Additionally, our community comprises members who attend
one Sunday service exclusively, often without much opportunity to interact with those
from the other service. We seek ways to establish personal connections between these

2
two groups through parish activities, including adult formation activities between
services and at other times of the week, outreach and other projects.
3. The rector will be a servant leader, helping St. Andrew’s grow and develop our lay
ministry. The rector will help us explore, identify, and document our talents, gifts, and
skills as individuals and as a parish community. Although our hearts are willing and
eager, we need assistance to further develop and foster leadership of our lay ministries.
More parish participation would imbue these ministries with vitality. The rector will help
us direct our gifts, talents, and skills toward needs within the parish and in the broader
community, doing the work God sends us to do.
4. The rector will have leadership strength and a sense of humor. They will oversee
administration and help us remain in compliance with all Diocesan policies. They will
empower and support the staff, vestry, and congregation to be engaged in lay
ministries, freeing the rector to use their time within the parish and out in community.

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value: At St. Stephen’s we aim to be the hands, feet, and face of God in our community. We envision a priest in partnership with us, forming and equipping us (and challenging us) to move deeper into our connections to God and our neighbors. We believe a part-time priest would be able to provide oversight and organization that we do not currently have. St. Stephen's does a lot of good for the community of Newton, and yet sometimes we step on each other's toes and get in one another's way. We have a tendency to be like the people of God in the book of Judges, "Every man did what was right in his own eyes". Our hope is that a collaborative priest partner with congregational development skills would help us work to be a more cohesive and effective witness to the Gospel and participate more collectively in service of God’s mission in Newton.

fields.value: The Episcopal Church of the Good Shepherd is a historic and beloved parish in Maitland, FL, with roots going back to 1883. Our property is a significant asset, encompassing our cherished historic chapel, a spacious Sanctuary built in 1967, a Parish Hall, and several outbuildings currently leased to the Celandine Life Prep Academy, a special needs school that brings meaningful weekday activity and community presence to our campus. Maitland is a thriving suburban community of approximately 20,000 residents located within the greater Orlando area, and it is experiencing steady, ongoing growth. That growth is reflected in new apartment construction near our church, bringing with it an influx of younger residents — exactly the demographic a vital, forward-looking parish seeks to engage. We see this as a genuine and exciting opportunity. Good Shepherd is beginning to take intentional steps to reach out to these newer neighbors, and we are eager for a Rector who shares our vision and will help lead that effort. While we are located in an area served by several churches, including two other Episcopal congregations within a few miles, we believe our history and our welcoming community give us a compelling foundation from which to grow. We are looking for a Rector who is energized by opportunity — someone ready to build on what we have and help shape what Good Shepherd will become.

Please contact Canon Ellis Brust, Canon for Clergy in Transition, for more information regarding this position. 
Email: ebrust@cfdiocese.org
Phone: 772-302-9661

fields.label: What are your community's hopes for this position, and what qualities in applicants will help you live into them?
fields.value: The top request from our congregation regarding our new Rector is someone who is effective at preaching and enjoys this aspect of their ministry. We hope for a Rector who can regularly share the Gospel in a relatable way, so our members can learn how their lives fit into God’s loving plan for His church and His people and join together to accomplish God’s plan for Good Shepherd and our surrounding community. Next, but still highly requested, we are seeking a visionary strategic leader — someone who can look at this parish in this neighborhood and help us see a compelling future. We are looking for a Rector who can help us develop a long-term vision and strategy for Good Shepherd and communicate it in a way that brings our membership along — inspiring ownership, commitment, and shared momentum. We are hoping for someone with skill in outreach and member development — particularly the ability to attract and retain young families. The younger residents moving into the apartments surrounding our campus represent a real opportunity, and we are looking for a Rector who is energized by that opportunity. Experience in connecting with younger demographics, understanding what draws young families to a parish, and helping us build the programming and relationships that keep them engaged will be an important skill. We are looking for someone with a pastor's heart — attentive to the spiritual, emotional, and physical wellbeing of our members and committed to the kind of personal care that has been central to Good Shepherd's identity. Our congregation is a family, and we hope our next Rector can genuinely love and tend that family even as they lead it forward. We hope our next rector would be comfortable speaking to a congregation having a broad theological understanding — someone who can respect our close-knit, community while gently and skillfully expanding our openness to change. This requires theological range and also some amount of relational intelligence and empathy. Finally, we are looking for a leader with the wisdom to guide adaptive change — someone who understands that moving a community forward requires patience, honesty, and skill, not just enthusiasm. This next chapter calls for a leader who has the courage and creativity to help us build what comes next. We are looking for a Rector who will find in Good Shepherd a community eager to grow, and who will consider it a privilege to help us do so.

fields.value:
La Iglesia Episcopal de Santiago, St. James Episcopal Church en Oakland está buscando un vicario para asumir las funciones de sacerdote para una pequeña congregación predominantemente latina en la diócesis de California. 
El candidato debe hablar español, estar familiarizado con el trabajo con pequeñas poblaciones urbanas, tener orientación multicultural y haber trabajado con poblaciones diversas. El
candidato debe estar teológicamente fundamentado en la fe de la iglesia pero flexible y tolerante a múltiples perspectivas y respetuoso de los demás. Al candidato debe gustarle trabajar con niños y estar abierto a incluirlos como parte del culto. El candidato debe sentir una llamada sagrada a ministrar a una comunidad de fe con potencial de crecimiento pero con pocos recursos materiales. 

El candidato seleccionado será igualitario y capaz de trabajar en colaboración en la ejecución de las siguientes tareas.
Sacerdotal: 1. Planificación de la liturgia; 2. Dirigir un culto dominical bilingüe cada semana; 3. Administrar los sacramentos; 4. Atención pastoral a la comunidad.

Programática: 1. Dirigir la formación y el desarrollo espiritual; 2. Formar miembros y desarrollar recursos dentro de la comunidad eclesial; 3. Gestionar y establecer relaciones con socios de la comunidad, como Permacultura Indígena, Cunamacué, la Asociación Liberiana y conexiones con la comunidad en general.

Administración: 1. Supervisar el trabajo del personal que incluye un administrador a tiempo parcial, un músico y un sacristán; 2. Supervisar las finanzas de la Iglesia y, en colaboración con el contable y el Comité Episcopal, preparar el presupuesto anual; 3. Trabajar con el Comité Episcopal para gestionar las instalaciones y la propiedad.

Thank you for your interest in the part-time (20 hours per week) Vicar position at La Iglesia Episcopal de Santiago/St. James Episcopal Church in Oakland, California.
Please contact Canon Denise Obando, at deniseo@diocal.org if you would like to learn more about this church and the position.
To apply for this position, submit a cover letter including why you feel called to this position, your current Vocation Hub profile, your resume and links to two recordings of sermons via email to deniseo@diocal.org.

Gracias por su interés en el puesto de Vicario a tiempo parcial (20 horas por semana) en La Iglesia Episcopal de Santiago/St. James Episcopal Church en Oakland, California.
Por favor, póngase en contacto con Canon Denise Obando, en deniseo@diocal.org si desea obtener más información sobre esta iglesia y la posición.
Para solicitar este puesto, envíe una carta de presentación que incluya por qué se siente llamado a este puesto, su portafolio de Vocation Hub, su currículum vitae y enlaces a dos
grabaciones de sermones por correo electrónico a deniseo@diocal.org.
